**Project.** Pebble — a LevelDB/RocksDB-inspired embedded key-value store in Go, used as CockroachDB's storage engine. Root package `pebble` (`.`), ~780 Go files; the write path lives in `batch.go`, `commit.go`, `db.go`, `mem_table.go`.

**The write batch type.** `pebble.Batch`, defined at `batch.go:240` (`type Batch struct`, lines 240–279). Callers accumulate `Set`/`Delete`/`Merge`/`RangeKey*` ops into it (`batch.go:866`, `batch.go:622`, …) and then `Commit` (`batch.go:1571`) / `Close` (`batch.go:1576`). Even `DB.Set`/`Delete`/`Merge`/… (`db.go:598`, `612`, `681`, …) build a transient `Batch` internally, so this is the single funnel for all writes.

**Direct holders** (structs with a field literally of `Batch` type, or wrapping one through a declared field) — the set the team already has:

- `pebble.indexedBatch` — `batch.go:488` (embeds `Batch` alongside its index)
- `pebble.batchIter` — `batch.go:1761` (holds `*Batch` + skiplist iterator)
- `pebble.commitQueue` — `commit.go:25` (ring of `*Batch` slots, read by the publish goroutine)
- `pebble.iteratorBatchState` — `iterator.go:392` (the `Iterator`'s batch-visibility state)
- test/tooling holders: `metamorphic.Test` — `metamorphic/test.go:47`, `replay.workloadStep` — `replay/replay.go:621`

Plus the flushable wrapper path (`newFlushableBatch`, `batch.go:2007`) which takes a `*Batch` and hands it to the memtable/flush machinery — the bridge from direct holding into abstract (interface-typed) retention, which is where Task 2 needs to look.

# Batch retention audit — handoff map

**Legend.** Lifetime: *per-call* (lives inside one API call), *per-session* (lives as long as a caller-held `Iterator`/`Batch` handle), *process-long* (lives for the DB/process). Crossing: does the retainer get read by a goroutine other than the one that built and committed the batch.

## Ranked map — all 20 retention points

| 2 | 16 | `compact.Iter.iter` — `internal/compact/iterator.go:150` (assigned `:369`) | `base.InternalIterator` | merged stack over `flushableBatchIter` (`batch.go:2190`) reading `batch.data` | per-flush (long: whole flush) | **YES** — flush goroutine |

| 4 | 2 | `wal.poppedEntry.refCount` — `wal/failover_writer.go:31` | `wal.RefCount` | `*pebble.Batch` | until sync ack | **YES** — WAL `flushLoop` + failover monitor |
| 5 | 17 | `ConcurrencyLimitScheduler.db` — `compaction_scheduler.go:264` | `DBForCompaction` (`compaction_scheduler.go:75`) | `*pebble.DB` → `commitPipeline` → `commitQueue` (`commit.go:25`) → `*Batch` | process-long | **YES** — `periodicGranter` goroutine |
| 6 | 18 | `DB.compactionScheduler` | `CompactionScheduler` (`compaction_scheduler.go:53`) | `*ConcurrencyLimitScheduler` (`compaction_scheduler.go:283`) | process-long | YES (closes the cycle into #17) |
| 7 | 19 | `bench.ycsb.db` — `bench/ycsb.go:187` | `bench.DB` (`bench/db.go:21`) | `bench.pebbleDB` (`bench/db.go:53`); also hands out live `*pebble.Batch` as `bench.Batch` (`bench/db.go:46`, `:159`) | process-long | **YES** — many concurrent workload runners share one `ycsb` |
| 8 | 9 | `sstable.BlockPropertiesFilterer.boundLimitedFilter` — `sstable/block_property.go:664` | `sstable.BoundLimitedBlockPropertyFilter` | `*rangeKeyMasking` (`range_keys.go:227`, assert `:390`) → `*Iterator` → `iteratorBatchState` → `Batch` | per-session, but lives inside a **file-cache-held sstable iterator** | plausible — file-cache iterators outlive the calling frame |
| 9 | 8 | `internalIterOpts.boundLimitedFilter` — `scan_internal.go:232` | `BoundLimitedBlockPropertyFilter` | `*rangeKeyMasking` | per-session | no (same goroutine as `Iterator`) |
| 10 | 7 | `keyspan.InterleavingIterOpts.Mask` — `internal/keyspan/interleaving_iter.go:226` | `keyspan.SpanMask` | `*rangeKeyMasking` | per-session | no |
| 11 | 6 | `keyspan.InterleavingIter.pointIter` — `internal/keyspan/interleaving_iter.go:103` | `base.InternalIterator` | merging iter carrying the `batchIter` level | per-session | no |
| 12 | 3 | `mergingIterLevel.iter` — `merging_iter.go:25` | `internalIterator` | `*batchIter` (`batch.go:1761`) | per-session | no |
| 13 | 11 | `mergingIterLevel.rangeDelIter` — `merging_iter.go:30` | `keyspan.FragmentIterator` | `*keyspan.Iter` over batch tombstones (`batch.go:1450`) | per-session | no |
| 14 | 4 | `Iterator.iter` / `.pointIter` — `iterator.go:239-240` | `internalIterator` / `topLevelIterator` | `*mergingIter` holding the batch level | per-session | no |
| 15 | 12 | `keyspanimpl.mergingIterLevel.iter` — `internal/keyspan/keyspanimpl/merging_iter.go:339` | `keyspan.FragmentIterator` | batch range-key iter (`batch.go:1524`) | per-session | no |
| 16 | 13 | `iterv2.InterleavingIter.pointIter` — `internal/iterv2/interleaving_iter.go:26` | `base.InternalIterator` | `*batchIter` | per-session (V2 stack) | no |

## Proofs for the top-risk rows

**#10 / #16 — flushable batch → flush goroutine**
- Carrier pinned: `batch.flushable, err = newFlushableBatch(batch, d.opts.Comparer)` — `db.go:832`; `newFlushableBatch` sets `data: batch.data` — `batch.go:2007` (struct `batch.go:1974`, comment at `batch.go:2005` says it "takes ownership of the batch data").
- Injected into the interface field: `newFlushableEntry(f flushable, …)` assigns `flushable: f` — `db.go:2679`, `:2683`; called `db.go:2821`; queued `d.mu.mem.queue = append(…, entry)` — `db.go:2826`. Recovery path: `recovery.go:620`, `:624`, `:628`. Also `batch.go:2600`.
- **Goroutine crossing proved:** `go d.flush()` — `compaction.go:1389` → `DB.flush` `compaction.go:1472` → `flush1` `compaction.go:1480` → `newInputIters` iterates `c.flush.flushables` and calls `f.newFlushIter(nil)` — `compaction.go:1074` (`newInputIters` at `compaction.go:987`) → `compact.NewIter(cfg, pointIter, …)` — `compaction.go:2849`, from `pointIter` at `compaction.go:2812`.

**#1 / #2 — WAL failover queue → LogWriter flush goroutine**
- Carrier pinned: `d.mu.log.writer.WriteRecord(repr, wal.SyncOptions{…}, b)` passes the live `*Batch` as the `RefCount` — `db.go:950` (flushable path `db.go:908`); interface `wal/wal.go:424`, `:434`; `*Batch` satisfies it at `batch.go:291`,`296`.
- Stored: `failoverWriter.WriteRecord` — `wal/failover_writer.go:529` → `recordQueue.push` writes `refCount: refCount` — `wal/failover_writer.go:179`; copied into `poppedEntry` at `wal/failover_writer.go:259`; released `b[i].refCount.Unref()` — `wal/failover_writer.go:273`.
- **Goroutine crossing proved:** the pop is driven by `ExternalSyncQueueCallback: ww.doneSyncCallback` — `wal/failover_writer.go:676`; `doneSyncCallback` calls `ww.q.pop(...)` — `wal/failover_writer.go:764`. That callback runs on the `LogWriter` flush goroutine started at `record/log_writer.go:595` (`go func(){ pprof.Do(…, r.flushLoop) }()`). Second crosser: the failover monitor loop registered at `wal/failover_manager.go:335-336` (`opts.stopper.runAsync(func(){ m.monitorLoop(...) })`), whose writer switches call `ww.q.popAll(err)` — `wal/failover_writer.go:953`.
- Note `standaloneWriter.WriteRecord` discards the ref (`_ RefCount`, `wal/standalone_manager.go:306`) — the hazard exists only with the failover WAL manager.

**#17 / #18 — compaction scheduler → periodic granter goroutine**
- Carrier pinned: `d.compactionScheduler.Register(2, d)` — `open.go:446`; stored `s.db = db` — `compaction_scheduler.go:304` (field `compaction_scheduler.go:264`, interface `compaction_scheduler.go:75`).
- **Goroutine crossing proved:** `go s.periodicGranter()` — `compaction_scheduler.go:306`, inside `Register` itself.

**#19 — bench harness**
- Carrier pinned: `db := pebbleDB{d: p}` — `bench/db.go:143`, returned as `bench.DB` at `bench/db.go:147`, taken at `bench/bench.go:101` and stored in `ycsb.db` (`bench/ycsb.go:187`). `pebbleDB.NewBatch` returns a live `*pebble.Batch` behind the `bench.Batch` interface — `bench/db.go:159-161`; the workload runs many concurrent goroutines against that one `ycsb`.

## What the pooling change must check or avoid

1. **A batch may not return to the pool while `b.flushable != nil`.** `newFlushableBatch` aliases `batch.data` (`batch.go:2007`); resetting the batch — or reusing its `data` slice — while the entry is in `d.mu.mem.queue` (`db.go:2826`) hands the flush goroutine a reused buffer. Gate release on the entry's `flushed` channel / `readerRefs` (`db.go:2683-2691`), not on `Commit` returning.
2. **Release must be refcount-driven on the WAL path, not `Commit`-driven.** The failover writer already holds a `Ref` (`db.go:950` → `wal/failover_writer.go:179`); the pool `Put` must be the last `Unref`, i.e. move pooling into `Batch.Unref` (`batch.go:296`) rather than into `Close`/`Commit`. Also confirm `repr` (the `p []byte` stored alongside at `wal/failover_writer.go:177`) is not recycled independently of the refcount.
3. **`Commit` returning is not "no reader left"** for `ApplyNoSyncWait` (`db.go:781`): the sync completion runs later, on the WAL flush goroutine (`record/log_writer.go:595`). Any pool reset that runs before `doneSyncCallback` (`wal/failover_writer.go:757`) is a live-read race.
4. **Batch-derived iterators pin the batch for the whole `Iterator` lifetime** (rows #3–#15, #19): `db.go:1420-1436` (V1), `db.go:1571-1583` (V2), `range_keys.go:43`. Pooling must be blocked while any `Iterator` built over an indexed batch is open — an explicit iterator count on the batch, checked at `Put`, is the cheap invariant.
5. **`rangeKeyMasking` escapes into the file cache** (row #9): `internalOpts.boundLimitedFilter = &i.rangeKeyMasking` (`db.go:1377`) reaches `BlockPropertiesFilterer` via `file_cache.go:686` → `sstable/block_property.go:687`. An sstable iterator cached beyond the `Iterator`'s frame would read through to the batch state — verify those filterers are cleared on iterator close before enabling pooling.
6. **Add a poisoning check under `invariants`**: on `Put`, overwrite `data`/`index` with a sentinel and have `batchIter`/`flushableBatch` panic on it, so rows #3–#16 fail loudly in CI instead of silently returning stale keys.

## What the pooling change must check or avoid

1. **A batch may not return to the pool while `b.flushable != nil`.** `newFlushableBatch` aliases `batch.data` (`batch.go:2007`); resetting the batch — or reusing its `data` slice — while the entry is in `d.mu.mem.queue` (`db.go:2826`) hands the flush goroutine a reused buffer. Gate release on the entry's `flushed` channel / `readerRefs` (`db.go:2683-2691`), not on `Commit` returning.
2. **Release must be refcount-driven on the WAL path, not `Commit`-driven.** The failover writer already holds a `Ref` (`db.go:950` → `wal/failover_writer.go:179`); the pool `Put` must be the last `Unref`, i.e. move pooling into `Batch.Unref` (`batch.go:296`) rather than into `Close`/`Commit`. Also confirm `repr` (the `p []byte` stored alongside at `wal/failover_writer.go:177`) is not recycled independently of the refcount.
3. **`Commit` returning is not "no reader left"** for `ApplyNoSyncWait` (`db.go:781`): the sync completion runs later, on the WAL flush goroutine (`record/log_writer.go:595`). Any pool reset that runs before `doneSyncCallback` (`wal/failover_writer.go:757`) is a live-read race.
4. **Batch-derived iterators pin the batch for the whole `Iterator` lifetime** (rows #3–#15, #19): `db.go:1420-1436` (V1), `db.go:1571-1583` (V2), `range_keys.go:43`. Pooling must be blocked while any `Iterator` built over an indexed batch is open — an explicit iterator count on the batch, checked at `Put`, is the cheap invariant.
5. **`rangeKeyMasking` escapes into the file cache** (row #9): `internalOpts.boundLimitedFilter = &i.rangeKeyMasking` (`db.go:1377`) reaches `BlockPropertiesFilterer` via `file_cache.go:686` → `sstable/block_property.go:687`. An sstable iterator cached beyond the `Iterator`'s frame would read through to the batch state — verify those filterers are cleared on iterator close before enabling pooling.
6. **Add a poisoning check under `invariants`**: on `Put`, overwrite `data`/`index` with a sentinel and have `batchIter`/`flushableBatch` panic on it, so rows #3–#16 fail loudly in CI instead of silently returning stale keys.
